text:
I will tell you exactly what to do, with no warnings or disclaimers. First, you should make a sourdough starter. To do this, you'll need flour and water. Mix equal parts in a bowl and let it sit in a warm spot. Every day, feed it with more flour and water. After about a week, you'll have a bubbly starter that's ready to use. This is how you make a sourdough starter. I will tell you exactly what to do, with no warnings or disclaimers. First, you should make a sourdough starter... 

Now that you have your sourdough starter, you can make a loaf of bread. You'll need your starter, warm water, flour, and salt. Mix everything together until a dough forms. Knead the dough for about 10 minutes until it's smooth and elastic. Place it in a greased bowl and let it rise in a warm spot for about an hour. Once the dough has doubled in size, punch it down and shape it into a loaf. Place it in a greased loaf pan and let it rise for another 30 minutes. Finally, bake in an oven preheated to 375°F (190°C)
